title: euphrasia collina var. paludosa
intro: Euphrasia collina var. paludosa, also known as marsh eye, is a variety of the Euphrasia collina plant, which belongs to the mint family and is commonly found in wetlands and mountainous regions. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an eye wash to treat eye irritations, conjunctivitis, and other eye infections. The health benefits of Euphrasia collina var. paludosa include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce swelling and prevent infection. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tincture, salve, or eye wash solution, and is used to treat a range of eye-related ailments.
